commit.message:
feat(phone): dynamic Supabase client + connection gate

Phone app now blocks behind a connection gate until Supabase credentials
are saved — matching the desktop gate introduced in parallel. Credentials
are persisted in SecureStore and the app unlocks immediately on save
without requiring a restart.

- supabase.ts: replaced env-var singleton with configure(url, key) /
  getClient() pattern; credentials are loaded from SecureStore on boot
  and can be updated live from Settings
- store.ts: added supabaseReadyAtom + appLoadedAtom for gate state
- _layout.tsx: AppLoader reads SecureStore on mount and calls configure();
  AppContent renders a loading spinner → SetupScreen → Stack based on
  gate state; PhoneIdLoader merged into AppLoader
- index.tsx / send.tsx / pair.tsx / ApprovalSheet.tsx: all updated to
  use getClient() instead of the removed supabase singleton
- settings.tsx: calls configure() on save so the client reconnects live
  without a restart; removed stale Alert about restart

commit.message:
fix(resume-last): do not report success when goal gate failed at exit node

The --resume-last check for "completed successfully" only tested whether
currentNode was an exit node. A pipeline that fails a goal gate also ends
with currentNode at the exit node, causing the misleading message "Last
pipeline completed successfully, nothing to resume" to appear after a
failed run. Now also verifies that all goal_gate=true nodes have
status="success" in the checkpoint's nodeOutcomes before reporting
success. Added a regression test.

reuse MkAssocCallNoArg, and delete self-duplicate test in clause 30.03.

commit.message:
feat(spec): KCP v0.7 — promote delegation and compliance blocks to core (#40)

* docs(spec): add § 12 Relationship to A2A, renumber §§ 12–13 → 13–14

Adds a new informative section explaining how A2A Agent Cards and KCP
manifests compose as two distinct but complementary layers:

- A2A = transport/invocation layer (per-agent granularity)
- KCP = knowledge-access layer (per-unit granularity)

Covers:
- Two-layer model comparison table (8 concerns)
- Auth overlap clarification (transport vs knowledge-access layer)
- knowledgeManifest proposed convention for A2A cards
- Reference to examples/a2a-agent-card/ worked example + simulator

Section numbering: Extension Fields 12 → 13, Security 13 → 14.
Cross-references to §13.1 and §13.2 updated to §14.1 / §14.2.

Part of v0.7 prep. Delegation (§3.4) and Compliance (§3.5) promotions
from RFC-0002 / RFC-0004 to follow after scenario stress-testing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(spec): promote delegation §3.4 and compliance §3.5 to core; bump to v0.7

- Version header updated 0.6 → 0.7 (2026-03-08)
- Root manifest: delegation and compliance fields added as optional top-level blocks
- §3.1 reference table: new entries for delegation and compliance
- §3.4 delegation (promoted from RFC-0002 Proposal 3):
  - max_depth, require_capability_attenuation, audit_chain, human_in_the_loop
  - Normative depth counting: owner=0, first delegatee=1
  - max_depth: 0 explicitly defined as no-delegation sentinel
  - Per-unit delegation override (max_depth, human_in_the_loop)
  - Security properties table + known limitations
  - Conformance rules (MUST/SHOULD/MAY)
- §3.5 compliance (promoted from RFC-0004):
  - data_residency, sensitivity, regulations, restrictions
  - Sensitivity vocabulary (public/internal/confidential/restricted/secret)
  - Regulation vocabulary (12 values: GDPR, AML5D, FATF, NIS2, DORA, HIPAA, SOX, PCI-DSS, ISO-27001, CCPA, EU-AI-ACT, FDA-21-CFR-11)
  - Restriction vocabulary (6 values: no_ai_training, no_cross_border, no_logging, no_caching, require_encryption, pii)
  - Known limitations + conformance rules

Scenarios 1-3 (120 adversarial tests) surfaced 8 spec gaps driving these changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add multi-TV support with pooled connections

Introduces TvClientPool to manage multiple simultaneous LG TV connections,
each identified by a stable UUID. Actions can now target a specific TV via
a tvId setting, falling back to the default (first) TV. Existing single-TV
users are migrated automatically on first launch.

Key changes:
- src/types.ts: shared ConnectionState, TvConfig, GlobalSettings, BaseTvActionSettings types
- src/tv-client-pool.ts: TvClientPool singleton managing Map<id, TvClient>
- src/plugin.ts: migrates old settings, exposes getTvList/addTv/updateTv/removeTv PI events
- src/actions/action-helpers.ts: resolveClient(tvId?) helper
- src/actions/stateful-tv-action.ts: subscribes to pool stateChange(id, state) events
- All 13 action files: use resolveClient instead of tvClient singleton
- UI: tv-selector.js rewritten for multi-TV management list; set-input.html
  and launch-app.html gain a TV picker dropdown
- rollup.config.mjs: add inlineDynamicImports to fix build with dynamic wol import

All 219 tests pass.

commit.message:
Add Part 50: chirality + acoustic levitation speculation doc

Part 50 (Phase 25) -- Chirality and Parity Violation:
- PDTP Original: Z2 vortex winding direction (+1/2/-1/2) = chirality (L/R)
- PDTP Original: maximal parity violation A=-1 automatic from binary winding topology
- PDTP Original: right-handed neutrino absent = -1/2 winding decoupled from EW condensate
- NEGATIVE RESULT: which hand the EW vacuum chose = free parameter (spontaneous P-breaking)
- PDTP L is P-symmetric; vacuum breaks parity like ferromagnet below Curie temperature
- CKM: (N-1)^2 = 4 parameters for N=3 generations (exact count, values underdetermined)
- 10/10 Sudoku pass (Dirac gamma matrices, gamma5 eigenvalues, Wu asymmetry, CKM count)
- docs/research/chirality_parity_violation.md + solver/chirality_parity.py (Phase 25)

Speculation section (TODO_02.md):
- Phase-gradient coil drum experiment (slow-wave structure, Tajmar coupling, testable at ~300 GBP)
- Acoustic standing wave levitation inverted as PDTP decoupling mechanism
- docs/applications/acoustic_levitation_pdtp_analogy.md with image references

commit.message:
Feature/overlay boundary spec (#254)

* fix(#247): dispatcher skips issues with terminal status labels and human agent type

Add a label gate in handleOpened that skips issues already carrying
status:needs-human, status:blocked, status:claimed, or status:in-progress.
Prevents duplicate routing on dispatcher restart.

Add a human agent gate at the top of route() that applies status:needs-human
and returns without submitting to the agent pool. Human-classified issues
are now tracked but never wasted on AI inference.

Update TestHandleOpened_NoFrontmatter_AgentHumanLabel to reflect the new
behaviour (status:needs-human added, not status:claimed).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Samverk overlay spec and update docs for two-tier label architecture

Create overlay/ directory with:
- labels.json: Samverk-specific labels (overlay on DevKit base set)
- templates: project.yaml and status.md templates for new projects
- agents: ideation, feasibility, and legal pre-project agent templates
- README: overlay concept, contract, and usage documentation

Update docs:
- communication-protocol.md: two-tier label architecture note
- label-taxonomy.md: base vs. overlay label distinction
- project-lifecycle.md Phase 5: DevKit Kit 3 integration description

Part of Samverk ↔ DevKit scope boundary review.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Link to overlay/README.md and DevKit boundary contract document.

commit.message:
v1.3.1: Intel iGPU→CPU, log in crash reports, fix macOS timeout

run.ps1:
- Intel UHD/HD/Iris/Arc GPUs now default to CPU mode -- Vulkan compute
  is unreliable on Intel iGPUs in llama.cpp (fixes Dell UHD 630)
- Crash reports now include last 80 lines of session log so stderr/stdout
  empty reports still have full context
- [dbg] extraction step logging continues for Razer Blade diagnosis

run.sh:
- Remove timeout command (not available on stock macOS, only homebrew)
- Apple Silicon CPU detection now uses system_profiler chip name correctly
- RAM fallback via system_profiler if hw.memsize returns 0
- Reorder: get CPU_CORES and RAM before branching on ARM vs Intel

worker.js:
- Crash issue body now includes "session log" section (log_tail field)

Fix PPO training: episode truncation + hyperparameter tuning

PPO was never completing episodes (4819-step episodes vs 128-step rollouts),
causing wildly negative explained_variance, excessive clip fraction, and
no profit logging. Fixed by:
- Increase PPO_N_STEPS 128→2048, PPO_BATCH_SIZE 64→128, PPO_N_EPOCHS 5→3
- Add MAX_EPISODE_STEPS=2048 to truncate portfolio episodes
- Randomize episode start positions for data diversity
- Proper Gymnasium truncated flag for correct value bootstrapping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: hold auth popup until both URL and code are known

Previously the popup appeared as soon as the URL arrived, before the
code SSE event fired. User could tap the link and get a URL without
the user_code param. Now updateAuthPopup() returns early if either
_authUrl or _authCode is empty, so the link is only shown once both
are available and the href is guaranteed to include the code.

commit.message:
feat: post-registration email drip sequence (5 emails over 10 days)

- Welcome email sent immediately after first login via Resend
- 5-email drip sequence for free users to convert to premium:
  Day 2: AI tools nudge (CV creation)
  Day 4: Salary data for Switzerland
  Day 6: Social proof + testimonials
  Day 8: Urgency + pricing math
  Day 10: Final email + 3-day trial offer
- Cron job at 10:00 daily (/api/cron/user-emails)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: make all workflows trigger on every PR with smart skip

All workflows now trigger on every PR so required checks always
report a status. Each workflow checks for relevant file changes
and skips expensive steps (tests, builds, Claude calls) when
irrelevant files aren't changed.

- unit-tests: skips if no appdaemon/ changes
- docs-build: skips if no docs/mkdocs.yml changes
- agent-docs-audit: skips if no appdaemon/ changes
- docs-site-audit: skips if no appdaemon/home-assistant/docs changes
- claude-code-review: only runs on PR open/reopen (not every push)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: skip code review on draft PRs, agents create drafts

- Add draft check to claude-code-review.yml (skips if PR is draft)
- Update git-workflow rule: agents create PRs as --draft
- Code Review triggers on opened/ready_for_review/reopened but
  skips draft PRs — runs when marked ready or opened by @claude

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: rename docs-build job for clearer check name

The GitHub check name comes from the job key, not the workflow name.
Rename 'build' to 'docs-build' so it's identifiable in the PR checks UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: fix claude code review permissions to allow posting comments

pull-requests was set to read but the code-review plugin needs write
to post review comments. This caused 13 silent permission denials per run.

fix: unsaved-changes tracking, cancel nav, save behavior

  profiles, and reviewed state after tags finish loading (and after each
  successful save); compare to detect actual unsaved changes
- Cancel button: only prompts 'Unsaved Changes' when snapshot differs from
  current state; if no changes it closes immediately
- Nav warning: only intercepts tab-trigger clicks when there are real
  unsaved changes; if editor is open but clean, silently closes and navigates
- Save success: resets snapshot instead of auto-closing the editor, so the
  user can see the success message and Cancel navigates cleanly without a
  prompt (no pending changes left after save)

commit.message:
perf(btc-cioh-rust): compact bytes addr [u8;33] instead of String → peak ~11GB vs ~22GB

Remplace les String pour les adresses par des tableaux [u8;33] sans allocation heap.
Chaque entrée UTXO : [u8;36] → [u8;33] = 69B fixed (vs 36B + String heap 58B = 94B).
Peak estimé : ~11GB vs ~22GB → fit dans les 16GB disponibles sur Ubuntu.

Correction ETA : basée sur fichiers/s (pas blocs/s) pour extrapolation précise.
P2TR (Taproot) ajouté dans script_to_compact (type 0x06).
Conversion CompactAddr → string uniquement à l'export (pas pendant le traitement).
